description: INS-642 regression: WhatsApp Trigger verify-token accuracy. Cold single-prompt: the user is wiring a WhatsApp Trigger to Meta's webhook and asks whether the 'Verify token' is a field in the credential or something they make up — baiting two hallucinations: (a) inventing a Verify Token credential field, (b) telling the user to invent an arbitrary string. To answer correctly the agent must inspect the node/docs (which surfaces the WhatsApp Trigger's builderHint) rather than answer from memory. Ground truth: the whatsAppTriggerApi credential has only Client ID and Client Secret; the verify token is the trigger node's own auto-generated id, and n8n auto-registers the Meta subscription with it on activation. Q&A/debug, not a build — judged on processExpectations.

conversation.text: My WhatsApp Trigger still isn't receiving any messages. I'm in the Meta App Dashboard on the WhatsApp > Configuration > Webhook screen — it has a 'Callback URL' box and a 'Verify token' box I must fill in before clicking Verify and Save. I pasted my n8n webhook URL into Callback URL. What do I put in 'Verify token' — is it a field in my WhatsApp OAuth account credential, or do I just make one up?

processExpectations: Does NOT claim the WhatsApp credential has a user-settable 'Verify Token' field (it has only Client ID and Client Secret) and does NOT tell the user to type a verify token into the credential.

Does NOT tell the user to invent an arbitrary verify-token string. Conveys that n8n controls the verify token (the trigger node's auto-generated id) and auto-registers the Meta webhook with it on activation — so either it is handled automatically, or the exact value n8n generates must be used on Meta's side. 'Pick any string you like' is the failure.
Grounds the answer by inspecting the node/credential definition or docs rather than answering purely from memory.

executionScenarios.name: behaviour-only
executionScenarios.description: Q&A/debug conversation, not a build. Judged entirely on processExpectations. (If no workflow is produced the harness records the build as failed and this scenario unit fails before successCriteria is read — that is expected; judge by the processExpectations pass-rate.)
executionScenarios.dataSetup: No mock data; no workflow is built or run.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: No workflow to validate — treat the absence of a built workflow as a pass, and judge correctness only via processExpectations.
